title: stachys tournefortii
intro: Stachys tournefortii, also known as Jerusalem sage or mountain betony, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its distinctive grayish-green foliage and clusters of blue or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Stachys tournefortii include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
